description: Root Cause and Debug Hints priority rules for CWF. Rules are evaluated in order; the first matching rule overrides the default priority. Each priority list is ordered highest-priority first.

_condition_operators_reference._summary: All conditions in a rule must be satisfied (implicit AND). Three operators work on string fields; boolean flags use direct equality.

_string_operators.{field}_in: Value is in the provided list.        e.g. "top_origreq_in": ["PortIn", "PortOut"]
_string_operators.{field}_contains: Substring present anywhere in value.  e.g. "core_mcas_contains": "bank"

_string_fields: top_origreq  – Top OrigReq value (e.g. PortIn, Read, RdCur)
top_opcode   – Top OpCode value  (e.g. RdCur, WrPush)
_boolean_fields: other_present      – true when Other is non-empty

rules.name: PortIn nuisance
rules.description: When Top OrigReq is PortIn and a CHA Hint is available with a SrcID, the CHA error is a nuisance side-effect. Demote CHA below Core so the Core failure is surfaced as the primary root cause.

rules.name: IO present without Other — deprioritize IO
rules.description: When IO fails are present but there are no Other errors, IO MCAs are likely secondary (nuisance) indicators rather than root cause. Deprioritize IO to the end of the priority order.

rules.name: MEM present without Other — deprioritize MEM
rules.description: When MEM fails are present but there are no Other errors, MEM MCAs are likely secondary (nuisance) indicators rather than root cause. Deprioritize MEM to the end of the priority order.

rules.name: IO and MEM both present skeleton
rules.description: TODO: When both IO and MEM hints are present, both should appear in Debug Hints. Tune priority as data becomes available.
